title: In-universe
content:
{{Top|real}}
{{Youmay|the general term|Wookieepedia's [[Wookieepedia:Manual of Style#Perspective|Manual of Style rules]] on appropriate uses of in-universe and out-of-universe perspectives}}
The term "'''in-universe'''" refers to information presented in the context of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe itself, as opposed to "'''out-of-universe'''," information presented in the context of the real world. For instance, the in-universe reason that [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] has a scar on his chin is that it was the result of a fight with a knife-wielding thug on [[Dellalt/Legends|Dellalt]], while the out-of-universe reason he has it is that the actor who portrayed him, [[Harrison Ford]], has a scar on his chin from a car accident.

"In-universe" also refers to works presented as if they were written by authors within the ''Star Wars'' universe. In-universe works are often general-readership source material like [[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']] or [[roleplaying game]] books like ''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' and ''[[Platt's Smugglers Guide]]''. To maintain the conceit that they come from within the ''Star Wars'' universe, such works omit mention of ''Star Wars'' as films or as a media franchise, maintain ignorance of information from later in the timeline than the books are allegedly written, and often posit a fictional "[[:Category:Authors|author]]" to be the in-universe creator of the work in question.

{{Stub}}

==Major "authors" of in-universe works==
{{Incomplete_list|oou=1}}

===Canon===

*[[C-3PO]] ([[The Secrets of the Wookiees]])
*[[Harli Cogra]] ([[Chronicles of the Jedi]])
*[[Beaumont Kin]] ([[The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]])
*[[Hondo Ohnaka]] ([[The Secrets of the Bounty Hunters]])
*[[R0-GR]] ([[Droidography]])
*[[Rex]] ([[The Secrets of the Clone Troopers]])
*[[Darth Sidious]] ([[The Secrets of the Sith]])
*[[Luke Skywalker]] ([[The Secrets of the Jedi]])
*[[Eloc Throno]] (''[[Traveler's Guide to Batuu]]'')
*[[Strono Tuggs]] ([[The Official Black Spire Outpost Cookbook]], [[The Life Day Cookbook]])

===Legends===
*[[Ebenn Q3 Baobab/Legends|Ebenn Q3 Baobab]] ([[Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide (real-life book)|''Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide'']])
*[[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] (''[[Cracken's Rebel Field Guide]]'', ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'')
**Dossier for Airen Cracken (''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'')
*[[Tem Eliss]] (''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]'')
*[[Arhul Hextrophon/Legends|Arhul Hextrophon]] (''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]'', ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'')
*[[Mammon Hoole]] ([[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']])
* [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]] (''[[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope]]'', ''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'', ''[[Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi]]'', ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'')
*[[Platt Okeefe/Legends|Platt Okeefe]] (''[[Smuggler's Log]]'', ''[[Platt's Smugglers Guide]]'', ''[[Platt's Starport Guide]]'')
*[[Obo Rin]] (''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]'')
*[[Tionne Solusar]] ([[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']])
*[[Lyrre T'enna]] (''[[The Wildlife of Star Wars: A Field Guide]]'') {{C|Primary researcher}}
*[[Milanda Vorgan]] (''[[The Written Word]]'')
*[[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] ([[Book of Sith]]) (Compiler and co-author)
